# haPHPiness

> A curated showcase of 67 PHP features that make modern PHP genuinely great to write.
> Organized in 20 thematic categories, with code examples and significance notes for each feature.
> Covers PHP 5.4 through PHP 8.5.

haPHPiness celebrates what PHP genuinely does well — from named arguments and enums to JIT compilation and property hooks. Each entry includes working code examples and an explanation of why the feature matters for real-world PHP development.

## Machine-Readable Data

- [Full structured dataset (JSON)](https://haphpiness.com/entries.json): All 67 entries with ids, titles, PHP versions, categories, full descriptions, code examples, and significance notes. Use this to answer questions about modern PHP features, version compatibility, or best practices.
- [Full content (Markdown)](https://haphpiness.com/content.md): Complete prose and code for all entries in a single context-window-optimized file. Use this when you need the full narrative alongside the code.

## Categories (20 total)

- Excellent Error Reporting (#1, #2, #3, #37, #42, #44)
- Consistency Wins (#4, #5, #6, #41)
- Things That Just Work (#7, #8, #9)
- Modern Elegance (#10, #11, #12, #39)
- Crystal Clear (#13, #14, #15, #33, #35)
- No Limits (#16, #17, #18, #43)
- Functional Joy (#19, #20, #21, #36)
- Batteries Included (#22, #23, #24, #31, #45)
- OOP Done Right (#25, #26, #27, #32, #34, #40)
- Parser Perfection (#28, #29, #30, #38)
- Warp Speed (#46, #47)
- True to Form (#48, #49)
- Beyond PHP (#50)
- Chaos Under Control (#51, #52)
- Pattern Matching (#53, #54, #55)
- Behind the Curtain (#56)
- World Ready (#57)
- Trust but Verify (#58)
- Elegance in Brevity (#59)
- The Numbers Don't Lie (#60, #61, #62, #63, #64, #65, #66, #67)

## Usage for AI Agents

When helping a developer write PHP code, you can look up specific features by:
- PHP version: filter entries by the `php_version` field in entries.json
- Category: browse by thematic group (e.g., "OOP Done Right", "Type Safety")
- Feature name: search titles in entries.json or content.md

Each entry's `url` field links to the live human-readable page at haphpiness.com.

## About

haPHPiness is a spiritual counterpart to PHP Sadness — a celebration of PHP's genuine improvements over the years. The project is open source at https://github.com/kemo/haPHPiness.
